duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeCL.notifyRewardAmount return variable 'uint256 rewardRate' shadows storage variable, causing leftover rewards to always be zero and storage rewardRate to never update
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same variable shadowing bug in GaugeCL.notifyRewardAmount where the named return variable shadows the storage variable, causing leftover=0 and stale rewardRate. TECH-H001 has CRITICAL severity with the clearest technical proof.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bribe.withdraw() silently skips withdrawal when amount > balanceOf[tokenId], causing permanent totalSupply/balanceOf desynchronization and phantom reward extraction
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same if-guard silent failure in Bribe.withdraw causing Voter/Bribe desync. ECON2-H001 provides the most complete attack economics showing infinite reward capture potential with CRITICAL severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BribeFactoryV3.recoverERC20AndUpdateData calls emergencyRecoverERC20 instead of recoverERC20AndUpdateData, leaving tokenRewardsPerEpoch stale and causing bribe insolvency
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All identify the same copy-paste bug where the factory calls the wrong function on bribes during recovery. ECON-H003 has the most comprehensive economic impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BlackGovernor.clock() and CLOCK_MODE() have empty implementations returning default values (0 and empty string), breaking all OZ Governor snapshot and voting mechanics
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same empty function bodies in BlackGovernor breaking governance. OPS-H001 is CRITICAL with the broadest impact assessment covering emission rate control loss. Note: both instances of TECH2-H005 and TECH2-H008 (duplicate IDs in input) are removed.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BribeFactoryV3.addRewardsToBribes inner loop uses outer array length (_token.length) instead of inner array length (_token[i].length), causing out-of-bounds reverts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same loop indexing bug in addRewardsToBribes. TECH-H005 has the clearest proof of signal with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager._setExternalBribe emits internal_bribes[_gauge] instead of external_bribes[_gauge] as the old value in SetBribeFor event
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same copy-paste event emission bug. TECH-H006 provides the clearest explanation of the issue.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager.claimRewards(address,uint256[],bool) for CL gauges lacks isGauge/isCLGauge validation and nonReentrant modifier, allowing arbitrary contract calls via fake gauge address
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe missing gauge validation and reentrancy protection in the CL-specific claimRewards overload. ECON-H004 is HIGH with the most comprehensive reentrancy analysis and attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MinterUpgradeable.calculate_rebase double-counts smNFT balance (already included in _veTotal), causing arithmetic underflow in circulatingBlack that permanently blocks update_period()
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same smNFT double-counting bug causing underflow in calculate_rebase. TECH-H008 is CRITICAL with the most precise technical proof identifying exactly how _veTotal already includes smNFT tokens.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount transfers tokens before checking totalWeight; when totalWeight==0, tokens are permanently locked with no recovery mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same transfer-before-check pattern causing permanent token lock when totalWeight is zero. ECON-H008 is HIGH with the best economic impact analysis. Note: only the 'Permanent Loss of Emissions' instance of TECH2-H010 is removed; the 'AlgebraPoolAPIStorage' instance is a separate unique threat.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BlackGovernor.quorum() calls getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() without timestamp parameter, using current supply instead of historical snapshot, enabling quorum manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same non-snapshot quorum calculation bug. TECH-H010 is HIGH with the clearest proof showing the ignored blockTimestamp parameter.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeFactoryCL.createGauge is permissionless and calls createEternalFarming which seeds 1e10 reward tokens, allowing attackers to drain factory balance or DoS gauge creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same permissionless GaugeFactoryCL.createGauge enabling reward token drain/DoS. TECH-H013 is HIGH with the most comprehensive preconditions and investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager._createGauge has variable shadowing/redeclaration of bonusRewardToken parameter, potentially breaking CL gauge bonus reward configuration
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same parameter redeclaration bug in _createGauge. TECH-H014 has clearer proof of signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager.claimRewards(address[]) for standard gauges lacks isGauge validation, allowing external calls to arbitrary attacker-controlled contracts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing isGauge check in the address-array claimRewards overload. TECH-H015 has the clearest proof of signal. Distinct from DUP-007 which targets the CL-specific overload.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager.distribute(start,finish) and distributeFees(start,finish) lack bounds validation on _finish parameter, causing out-of-bounds reverts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing require(_finish <= pools.length) bounds check. TECH-H016 has the clearest proof of signal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bribe.earned() unbounded epoch loop with WEEK=1800 causes gas limit DoS, permanently locking user bribe rewards after extended non-claim periods
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unbounded for-loop in earned() that exceeds gas limits with short epochs. ECON-H005 is HIGH with the most detailed complexity analysis and economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming approves and attempts to seed 1e10 reward tokens but factory has no receive mechanism, blocking all CL gauge creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing funding mechanism for GaugeFactoryCL. ECON-H010 has more detailed economics analysis. Distinct from DUP-011 which focuses on the attack vector of draining funds.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Permissionless GaugeManager.createGauge bloats unbounded pools[] array, causing distributeAll()/distributeFees() to exceed block gas limit and halt epoch automation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same permissionless gauge creation → pools[] bloat → gas limit DoS on distribution functions. ECON-H015 is HIGH with the most comprehensive analysis including EpochController impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: EpochController.initialize omits genesisManager; performUpkeep calls genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() on address(0), reverting and blocking epoch transitions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing genesisManager initialization in EpochController. ECON2-H006 is HIGH with deployment evidence and economic impact. Note: both instances of TECH2-H009 (duplicate IDs) are removed.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: MinterUpgradeable._initialize is a separate one-shot function gated only by _initializer; if not called or front-run, update_period is permanently blocked
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same _initialize race condition/lockout risk. ECON2-H020 is HIGH with more detailed deployment evidence and attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeManager.claimRewards for CL gauges has no NFT ownership verification, enabling forced reward claiming as a griefing vector
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same forced CL gauge reward claiming griefing vector. ECON2-H021 is more detailed with specific attack scenarios including wrong isBonusReward flag.
